title: linaria heterophylla subsp. aurasiaca
intro: Linaria heterophylla subsp. aurasiaca, also known as the yellow-toothed toadflax, is a plant species in the figwort family that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. The medicinal properties of Linaria heterophylla subsp. aurasiaca include its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to relieve coughs, colds, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to soothe digestive problems and alleviate menstrual cramps. The herbal preparations of Linaria heterophylla subsp. aurasiaca include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
